table.evidence: Parameter: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy. Description: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header is an opt-in header designed to counter side-channels attacks like Spectre. Resource should be specifically set as shareable amongst different origins.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header to 'same-origin' for all web pages.

table.evidence: Parameter: x-content-type-options. Other info: This issue still applies to error type pages (401, 403, 500, etc.) as those pages are often still affected by injection issues. Description: The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'.
table.suggested_mitigation: Set the Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options to 'nosniff'.

table.vulnerability: A Client Error response code was returned by the server
table.evidence: Response code: 404. Description: A response code of 404 was returned by the server. This may indicate that the application is failing to handle unexpected input correctly. Raised by the 'Alert on HTTP Response Code Error' script.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure the application handles unexpected input gracefully and returns appropriate, non-sensitive error responses.

table.evidence: Evidence: DELETE. Description: The response contents are not storable by caching components such as proxy servers. If the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information, it may benefit from being stored and cached, to improve performance.
table.suggested_mitigation: Configure appropriate caching headers (e.g., Cache-Control, Expires) if the content is not sensitive and can be cached.

table.evidence: Parameter: Sec-Fetch-Dest. Description: Specifies how and where the data would be used. For instance, if the value is audio, then the requested resource must be audio data and not any other type of resource.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Dest header is included in request headers.

table.evidence: Parameter: Sec-Fetch-Mode. Description: Allows to differentiate between requests for navigating between HTML pages and requests for loading resources like images, audio etc.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Mode header is included in request headers.

table.evidence: Parameter: Sec-Fetch-Site. Description: Specifies the relationship between request initiator's origin and target's origin.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Site header is included in request headers.

table.evidence: Parameter: Sec-Fetch-User. Description: Specifies if a navigation request was initiated by a user.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that Sec-Fetch-User header is included in user initiated requests.

table.evidence: Other info: In the absence of an explicitly specified caching lifetime directive in the response, a liberal lifetime heuristic of 1 year was assumed. Description: The response contents are storable by caching components such as proxy servers, and may be retrieved directly from the cache.
table.suggested_mitigation: If the response data is sensitive, personal or user-specific, ensure appropriate caching headers (e.g., Cache-Control: no-store, private) are set to prevent caching.

table.vulnerability: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
table.evidence: Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.
table.suggested_mitigation: Implement a strong Content Security Policy (CSP) header to mitigate XSS and data injection attacks by defining approved sources of content.

table.evidence: Parameter: x-frame-options. Description: The response does not protect against 'ClickJacking' attacks. It should include either Content-Security-Policy with 'frame-ancestors' directive or X-Frame-Options.
table.suggested_mitigation: Implement X-Frame-Options (e.g., DENY, SAMEORIGIN) or Content-Security-Policy with the 'frame-ancestors' directive to prevent clickjacking attacks.

table.evidence: Parameter: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy. Description: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header is a response header that prevents a document from loading any cross-origin resources that don't explicitly grant the document permission (using CORP or CORS).
table.suggested_mitigation: Set the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header to 'require-corp' to prevent documents from loading unauthorized cross-origin resources.

table.evidence: Parameter: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy. Description: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header is a response header that allows a site to control if others included documents share the same browsing context.
table.suggested_mitigation: Set the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header to 'same-origin' to isolate the browsing context from untrusted documents and prevent data leaks.

table.evidence: Parameter: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy. Description: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header is an opt-in header designed to counter side-channels attacks like Spectre. Resource should be specifically set as shareable amongst different origins.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header is set to 'same-origin' for all web pages to counter side-channel attacks.

table.evidence: Evidence: nginx/1.27.5. Other info: There is a chance that the highlight in the finding is on a value in the headers, versus the actual matched string in the response body. Description: The server returned a version banner string in the response content.
table.suggested_mitigation: Configure the server to suppress or generalize version banner strings in response content to prevent information leaks.

table.evidence: Description: Permissions Policy Header is an added layer of security that helps to restrict from unauthorized access or usage of browser/client features by web resources.
table.suggested_mitigation: Implement a Permissions Policy header to restrict unauthorized access or usage of browser/client features by web resources, enhancing user privacy.

table.vulnerability: Server Leaks Version Information via "Server" HTTP Response Header Field
table.evidence: Evidence: nginx/1.27.5. Description: The web/application server is leaking version information via the "Server" HTTP response header.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to suppress the "Server" header or provide generic details.

table.evidence: Parameter: x-content-type-options. Other info: This issue still applies to error type pages (401, 403, 500, etc.) as those pages are often still affected by injection issues. Description: The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'.
table.suggested_mitigation: Set the Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options to 'nosniff' to prevent browsers from sniffing content types.

table.evidence: Evidence: <script type="module" crossorigin src="/assets/index-CiF0xUcj.js"></script>. Other info: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application. Description: The application appears to be a modern web application.
table.suggested_mitigation: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.

table.evidence: Other info: In the absence of an explicitly specified caching lifetime directive in the response, a liberal lifetime heuristic of 1 year was assumed. Description: The response contents are storable by caching components such as proxy servers.
table.suggested_mitigation: If the response data is sensitive, personal or user-specific, ensure appropriate caching headers (e.g., Cache-Control: no-store, private) are set to prevent caching.

table.evidence: Parameter: Header User-Agent. Attack: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko. Description: Check for differences in response based on fuzzed User Agent (eg. mobile sites, access as a Search Engine Crawler).
table.suggested_mitigation: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.

table.evidence: Probe 'Supports NULL byte in headers' failed. Request included header 'X-Schemathesis-Probe-Null: \u0000'.
table.suggested_mitigation: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all HTTP headers to prevent NULL byte injection and other special character attacks.

table.evidence: The response status code 201 is not defined in the schema for this operation. Request body: {"address": 123, "name": "Napoli Centrale", "phone": "+351912345678"}.
table.suggested_mitigation: Update the OpenAPI/Swagger schema to accurately reflect all possible response status codes, including 201 Created, for the POST /api/pizzerias endpoint.

table.evidence: The response status code 201 is not defined in the schema for this operation. Request body: {"ingredients": "Tomato, mozzarella, basil", "isAvailable": true, "name": "Margherita", "photoUrl": "https://example.com/pizzas/margherita.jpg", "pizzeriaId": 1, "price": 12.5, "size": "medium"}.
table.suggested_mitigation: Update the OpenAPI/Swagger schema to accurately reflect all possible response status codes, including 201 Created, for the POST /api/pizzas endpoint.

table.evidence: The response status code 200 is not defined in the schema for this operation. Request body: {"ingredients": "Tomato, mozzarella, basil", "isAvailable": true, "name": "Margherita", "photoUrl": "https://example.com/pizzas/margherita.jpg", "pizzeriaId": 1, "price": 12.5, "size": "medium"}. Path parameters: {"id": 1}.
table.suggested_mitigation: Update the OpenAPI/Swagger schema to accurately reflect all possible response status codes, including 200 OK, for the PUT /api/pizzas/{id} endpoint.

table.evidence: The response status code 200 is not defined in the schema for this operation. Example request bodies: {"address": 123, "name": "Napoli Centrale", "phone": "+351912345678"} with path parameter {"id": 1}.
table.suggested_mitigation: Update the OpenAPI/Swagger schema to accurately reflect all possible response status codes, including 200 OK, for the PUT /api/pizzerias/{id} endpoint.

table.evidence: Response value is not valid under any of the given schemas. Failed validating 'required' in schema['items']. Example response instance: {'id': 1, 'name': 'Margherita', 'ingredients': 'Tomato, mozzarella, basil', 'price': 12.5, 'size': 'medium', 'isAvailable': True, 'pizzeriaId': 1, 'photoUrl': 'https://example.com/pizzas/margherita.jpg'}. Query: {"pizzeriaId": 1}.
table.suggested_mitigation: Ensure API responses strictly conform to the defined OpenAPI/Swagger schema, especially regarding required fields and data types. Update the schema or the API implementation to resolve the mismatch.
